sound_type: massive detonation body-slam -- Southern Resident Killer Whale fully airborne, crashing back to water surface, ~165 dB impact, E=9
ecosystem: Open water -- Salish Sea, Strait of Juan de Fuca, open Pacific approaches
season: Resident (year-round for Southern Residents in Salish Sea)
milestone: E=9 TIES ABSOLUTE SPS MAXIMUM (with Lightning Strike at degree 260). ORCA ARC OPENS (degrees 270-276). COMBINED E=17 WITH S36 273 (The Refuzors) -- ENGINE APEX. First living creature to reach E=9 in engine.

coordinates.description: Southern Resident Killer Whale range: Salish Sea, San Juan Islands, Strait of Juan de Fuca, north to Gulf Islands BC. Primary summer range around San Juan Islands.

taxonomy_note.description: Southern Resident Killer Whale (SRKW): a distinct population segment of Orcinus orca. Resident ecotype: fish-eating (primarily Chinook salmon). Three pods: J (~23), K (~17), L (~33). ~73 total individuals. ESA endangered since 2005. Peak population ~98 in 1995. Not the same ecotype as transient (mammal-eating) orcas documented at degree 272.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Delphinidae entries at degree 270+: orca arc degrees 270-276
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Orcinus from Latin orca (a kind of whale, from orcus, the underworld -- the Romans associated the orca's appearance with the realm of the dead). orca: the killer whale. Common name 'killer whale' is a translation of the Spanish ballena asesina or orca asesina observed by sailors watching orcas hunt other cetaceans.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea (within Artiodactyla under modern phylogenetics). Whales, dolphins, and porpoises. Orcinus orca is the largest member of Delphinidae.

sound_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Broadband event -- breach impact is not tonal. Frequency range spans from low-frequency thumps (50-200 Hz) through mid-frequency water noise (200-2000 Hz) to higher-frequency spray sounds.
sound_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: Low-to-mid frequency: breach impact dominated by 50-500 Hz energy, with broadband white noise from splash and spray.
sound_metrics.sound_frequency_hz: Broadband. Not a vocalization -- a physical impact event.
sound_metrics.song_duration_s: Impact: approximately 0.1-0.5 seconds for primary detonation. Water splash and wave propagation: several seconds. Underwater propagation: continues for many seconds at distance.
sound_metrics.amplitude: E=9. ~165 dB at close range (within 10 meters). Comparable to: rifle shot at shooter position (~160 dB), jet engine at 30 meters (~150 dB). At 100 meters: drops significantly but remains remarkable. Propagates in air (audible kilometers in calm conditions) and underwater (speed ~1500 m/s).
sound_metrics.key_property: E=9 -- TIES ABSOLUTE MAXIMUM (with Lightning Strike, degree 260). First living creature in engine to reach E=9. Six-ton (adult male SRKW 5400-6000 kg) adult animal fully airborne, reaching 3-5 meters above surface, returning in lateral body-slam. Impact ~165 dB. Orca arc opens at 273. Combined E=17 with The Refuzors (S36 273) = ENGINE APEX.

physical_description.overall: Adult male Southern Resident Killer Whale: length 5.8-7.0 meters, mass 5,400-6,000 kg. Distinctive black-and-white coloration. Tall, straight dorsal fin (adult males up to 1.8 meters). White eye patch. Gray saddle patch behind dorsal fin -- individual variation allows photo-ID. Adult females: smaller, curved dorsal fin.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: For degree 273 (breaching): the silhouette of an orca fully airborne -- black-and-white body against sky or water, water cascade beneath it, the arch of the body in the air. The impact plume is visible from kilometers. The sound reaches even further.
physical_description.field_id: A breach from an adult male Southern Resident is unmistakable: the six-ton body, the tall straight dorsal fin, the black-and-white pattern, the enormous impact. Nothing in the Salish Sea produces this event.

behavioral_context.breach_function: Multifunctional -- communication (long-range underwater sound), ectoparasite removal (water-entry shear force), play/social bonding (socially contagious behavior), sensory exploration (aerial perspective). No single confirmed function. Energetically costly; unlikely to be purely casual.
behavioral_context.social_context: More frequent in socially excited contexts. Contagious: one animal breaches, others often follow. More common in certain individuals. Observed in all age classes. Cultural component: behavioral patterns transmitted within pods.
behavioral_context.frequency_of_behavior: Not constant -- bouts of breaching behavior occur, often multiple breaches in sequence from one or more individuals. More frequent in summer in primary SRKW range.

nodes.title: E=9 Ties Absolute Maximum -- Engine Apex
nodes.description: Second E=9 in engine history; first from a living creature (Lightning Strike at 260 was the first). Combined E=17 with The Refuzors (S36 273): engine apex. Highest combined energy in engine history.

nodes.title: Breach Physics -- Six Tons Fully Airborne
nodes.description: Adult male SRKW: 5,400-6,000 kg. Approach velocity 17-26 km/h. Maximum height 3-5 meters. Lateral body-slam impact: ~165 dB. Propagates in air and underwater. White water plume visible kilometers distant.

nodes.title: Southern Residents -- ~73 Individuals -- ESA Endangered 2005
nodes.description: J, K, L pods. Three primary threats: Chinook decline (cross-ref degree 237), vessel noise, PCB/PBDE contaminants. Among most contaminated marine mammals globally. Culturally sophisticated: pod dialects, multi-generational knowledge transfer.

nodes.title: Breach Function -- Multifunctional Behavior
nodes.description: Communication, ectoparasite removal, play/social bonding, sensory exploration. Contagious in social contexts. Too costly for pure play; context suggests multiple functions.

nodes.title: Orca Arc -- Seven Degrees (270-276)
nodes.description: Most sustained single-species run in SPS catalog. Degrees 270-276 all Orcinus orca. 273 is the energy maximum (E=9); 276 is the perceptual maximum (224 dB echolocation). Arc closes at 276.

nodes.title: Chinook Dependency -- Cross-Reference Degree 237
nodes.description: SRKWs are fish-eating; primary prey is Chinook salmon. Chinook decline is primary SRKW population threat. Degree 237 documents Chinook; degree 273-276 documents the orca that depends on it. Direct ecological link in engine structure.

cross_references.relationship: Only previous E=9 in engine. Orca breaching ties it. Abiotic maximum = biological maximum. Atmospheric electrical discharge vs. living mammal at same energy level.
SRKW primary prey. Chinook decline drives SRKW population crisis. Direct ecological dependency documented in engine: salmon at 237, orca at 273-276.
Orca arc beginning. Same species as vocal communicator (270) vs. physical force (273). Voice to body. Signature call to explosive breach.
Arc closing. Physical maximum (273 breach E=9) vs. perceptual maximum (276 echolocation 224 dB source). The arc's two extremes.
ENGINE APEX. E=17 combined. E=8 + E=9. Seattle hardcore 40 years at maximum energy + orca's most explosive behavioral moment.
